profile.name: Manmath Mohanty
profile.bio: CONTRIBUTING AND MAKING SOMETHING THAT HELPS OTHERS

prs.title: feat: Add HSM/offline signing support for multi-node transactions
prs.body:
Resolves #976

**Description**:

Add HSM/offline signing support for multi-node transactions, enabling workflows where private keys never leave secure hardware (e.g., Hardware Security Modules).

* Add `SignableNodeTransactionBodyBytes` header-only data class with `mNodeAccountId`, `mTransactionId`, and `mSignableTransactionBodyBytes` fields
* Add `getSignableNodeBodyBytesList()` virtual method to `Transaction` that extracts canonical bodyBytes per node from frozen transactions
* Add `getSignableNodeBodyBytesList()` override in `ChunkedTransaction` for chunked transaction support
* Add `HsmSigningExample.cpp` demonstrating the full HSM signing workflow
* Add 7 unit tests covering frozen/unfrozen state, node count, body bytes validation, and cross-type support

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #976

**Notes for reviewer**:

- Implementation is a 1:1 match with the [JS SDK reference](https://github.com/hiero-ledger/hiero-sdk-js/blob/main/src/transaction/Transaction.js#L1618-L1648) and follows the [design document](https://github.com/hiero-ledger/sdk-collaboration-hub/blob/main/proposals/manual-signature-hsm-design-proposal.md)
- SDK library compiles successfully (629/629 targets), all files pass `clang-format`
- `SignableNodeTransactionBodyBytes` is header-only following the `HbarAllowance` pattern
- **Known limitation**: The existing `addSignature()` enforces `requireOneNodeAccountId()`, so multi-node HSM signing requires using `signWith()` with a callback instead. This is a pre-existing constraint matching the JS SDK behavior, not introduced by this PR
- Integration tests (test plan items 1–5) require a live Hiero testnet and are expected to run in CI

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

prs.title: feat: Add operator== to ContractInfo (#1427)
prs.body:
Add operator== to ContractInfo following the PendingAirdropId member-function pattern.

Add [[nodiscard]] bool operator==(const ContractInfo&) const declaration in ContractInfo.h
Implement operator== in ContractInfo.cc, comparing all member fields:
Primitive fields: mContractId, mAccountId, mContractAccountId, mExpirationTime, mAutoRenewPeriod, mStorage, mMemo, mBalance, mIsDeleted, mLedgerId, mAutoRenewAccountId, mMaxAutomaticTokenAssociations
mAdminKey: null-safe comparison via toBytes()
mStakingInfo: inline field-by-field comparison (all 6 fields)
mTokenRelationships: map size + entry-by-entry comparison (all 7 fields)
Initialize mExpirationTime and mAutoRenewPeriod to deterministic defaults
Add 4 equality unit tests in ContractInfoUnitTests.cc
Related issue(s):

Fixes #1427

Notes for reviewer:

Follows the PendingAirdropId member-function pattern (bool operator==(...) const) rather than friend — the reference implementation in PendingAirdropId.h:81 uses this same approach.
mAdminKey is compared by byte value (toBytes()) rather than pointer identity, so two distinct Key objects with the same content are considered equal.
Uses !(a == b) instead of a != b for some SDK types (Hbar, AccountId, TokenId) because C++17 does not auto-synthesize operator!= from operator==.
All 5 ContractInfoUnitTests pass locally (including the pre-existing FromProtobuf test).
[==========] Running 5 tests from 1 test suite.
[  PASSED  ] 5 tests.

prs.title: Fix swapStatusLabel silently swallowing errors
prs.body:
Resolves #1441

**Description**:
Update `swapStatusLabel` to aggregate and return errors from underlying API calls instead of failing silently. Update its callers to check the return value and explicitly log any errors, preventing misleading success logs when a label swap fails due to permission issues.

* Update `swapStatusLabel` to return `{ success, errorDetails }`
* Log errors in `bot-on-pr-review.js` when label swap fails
* Log errors in `bot-on-pr-update.js` when label swap fails
* Log errors in `bot-on-pr-merged.js` when label swap fails
* Log errors in `bot-on-pr-open.js` when label swap fails

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #1441

**Notes for reviewer**:
All 41 existing integration tests in `.github/scripts/tests/` have been executed locally and continue to pass successfully. The updated code strictly follows the existing error-handling pattern already established in the `swapLabels` helper.

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

prs.title: chore: extend Dependabot coverage to vcpkg and document FetchContent …
prs.body:
Extend Dependabot coverage to `vcpkg` and add documentation for managing `FetchContent` pins.

* Add `vcpkg` package-ecosystem entry to `.github/dependabot.yml` (weekly schedule, 5 open PR limit)
* Create `docs/contributing/dependencies.md` documenting:
  * Automated monitoring via Dependabot for GitHub Actions and `vcpkg`
  * `FetchContent` pins: `log4cxx`, `HProto`, and `dotenv-cpp`
  * Quarterly manual review cadence recommendation
  * Step-by-step guide for updating `FetchContent` pins

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #1444

**Notes for reviewer**:
Dependabot officially added support for `vcpkg` recently, so we are now able to automate the `builtin-baseline` hash updates in `vcpkg.json`. I also found a third `FetchContent` pin (`dotenv-cpp` in the examples) and included it in the new documentation alongside `log4cxx` and `HProto`.

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [ ] Tested (unit, integration, etc.) *(N/A - Documentation and CI config changes only)*

prs.title: Fix/dynamic call reminder time

labels.name: skill: beginner
labels.description: Achievable by a fairly new comer that has already completed a couple of good first issues

labels.name: step: 2nd
labels.description: second stage of the review approval process

labels.name: step: merge ready
labels.description: PR is ready and waiting to merge

labels.name: reviewer: write
labels.description: requires a review with write permissions

prs.body:
**Description:**
Updates the GitHub cron "call reminder" bots to generate a dynamic "time until meeting" message at runtime (to account for delayed cron execution), replacing the hardcoded "approximately 4 hours" message.

Changes include:
*   Added `MEETING_HOUR` as a configurable, validated top-level variable in both `cron-calls-community.sh` and `cron-calls-office-hours.sh`.
*   Extracted the time-remaining computation logic into a shared helper script (`.github/scripts/compute-time-until-meeting.py`) to prevent duplication and ensure consistency. The logic correctly calculates hours and minutes remaining using `math.ceil` for accurate rounding.
*   Updated the posted reminder messages to dynamically display the computed time remaining and the configured meeting time.
*   Added a bash-based test script (`.github/scripts/test-cron-calls-time-computation.sh`) containing 10 test cases to verify the time computation logic (including edge cases, rounding, and pluralization).

**Related issue(s):** #2209

**Fixes #2209**

**Notes for reviewer:**
*   The Python helper supports an optional `MOCK_TIME` argument specifically designed for testing, enabling precise validation down to the second.
*   All 11 test cases in `test-cron-calls-time-computation.sh` are passing successfully.

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

prs.title: refactor: use createDelegatingLogger in bot-recommend-issues.js
prs.body:
## Description

Replaces the inline logger delegation pattern in `bot-recommend-issues.js` with the shared `createDelegatingLogger()` helper from `helpers/logger.js`.

This aligns the file with the pattern already used in `assign.js`, `unassign.js`, and `finalize.js`, reducing duplication and improving consistency across command modules.

## Changes

- Added `createDelegatingLogger` to the existing `require('../helpers')` destructure
- Replaced the inline logger object with `const logger = createDelegatingLogger()`
- No functional or behavioral changes — all existing `logger.log` and `logger.error` calls continue to work identically

## Testing

- All 30 existing unit tests pass ✅
- Module loads correctly with `require()`

Fixes #1361

prs.title: fix: remove 36 orphaned test separator comments across 8 unit test files
prs.body:
PR #1495 added section comment headers (e.g. // Constructor Tests) bordered by //----- lines. A follow-up commit removed those headers but only deleted the first two lines of each block, leaving the closing //----- border behind.

This produced a double-separator pattern at every former section boundary:

    //-----          ← orphaned closing border
                     ← blank line
    //-----          ← original test separator
    TEST_F(...)

Collapsed all 36 occurrences to the correct single-separator pattern:

    //-----
    TEST_F(...)

Files changed (orphaned separators removed):
  - AccountIdUnitTests.cc               (6)
  - NftIdUnitTests.cc                   (6)
  - TopicIdUnitTests.cc                 (6)
  - ScheduleIdUnitTests.cc             (5)
  - TokenIdUnitTests.cc                 (5)
  - EvmAddressUnitTests.cc             (3)
  - HbarUnitTests.cc                    (3)
  - MirrorNodeContractQueryUnitTests.cc (2)

No test logic, assertions, or production code was modified.

**Description**:
<!--
One or two line summary of what this PR does and why it is needed, followed by a list
of changes in imperative, present tense for use in the commit message or changelog. Example:

Add support for ...

* Add config property
* Change column name
* Remove ...
-->

**Related issue(s)**: #1501  #1501

Fixes #1501

**Notes for reviewer**:

- [ ] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

prs.title: chore: add return type annotation to _is_payment_required in CryptoGetAccountBalanceQuery

labels.name: step: 1st
labels.description: 1st stage of the review approval process

labels.name: reviewer: junior committer
labels.description: request review from junior committers with triage permissions

labels.name: reviewer: committer
labels.description: request review help from a committer

prs.body:
## Description
Add the missing `-> bool` return type annotation to the `_is_payment_required()` method in `CryptoGetAccountBalanceQuery`.
This was the only public/private method in the class without a return type annotation. The return type is unambiguous — the method always returns `False`, and the existing docstring already documents it as `bool`.
## Changes
- **File:** `src/hiero_sdk_python/query/account_balance_query.py`
- **Change:** `def _is_payment_required(self):` → `def _is_payment_required(self) -> bool:`
No other files, methods, or lines were modified. SDK behavior is unchanged.
## Testing
- All 13 unit tests in `tests/unit/account_balance_query_test.py` pass ✅
## Related Issue
Closes #2059

prs.title: feat: enhance test coverage for TransactionFactory (Access List & Address formatting)
prs.body:
### Description

This PR significantly improves the reliability of the pectra-upgrade branch by adding a comprehensive suite of unit tests for the newly introduced formatAccessList and formatAddress helpers in TransactionFactory.ts.

While the implementation for Pectra support was already present, these helpers were largely untested. This contribution fills that gap, ensuring that the normalization logic for access lists (required for EIP-2930, EIP-1559, and EIP-7702) is robust against raw data from the MirrorNode.

Expanded Test Coverage: I added 37 new unit tests to the transactionFactory.spec.ts, increasing the total from 24 to 61 tests. All tests are currently passing.
Improved Edge Case Handling: Covered multiple scenarios for formatAccessList and formatAddress, including handling of null/undefined inputs, short address padding, oversized address truncation, and correctly processing the EMPTY_HEX ("0x") string from MirrorNode.
Verified Propagation: Confirmed that the access_list is correctly propagated for EIP-2930, EIP-1559, and EIP-7702 transaction types.

### Solution

prs.title: feat: support unencoded access_list format from MirrorNode
prs.body:
## Description

Adds dual-format support for the `access_list` field from the MirrorNode `/contracts/results` endpoint. The MirrorNode is transitioning from returning `access_list` as an RLP-encoded hex string (e.g., `"0x"`) to an unencoded array of objects (`[{address, storage_keys}]`) per hiero-ledger/hiero-mirror-node#13343.

This PR ensures the relay handles both formats seamlessly during and after the transition.

## Changes

### `src/relay/lib/factories/transactionFactory.ts`
- Added `formatAccessList()` normalizer — handles both encoded (`"0x"`) and unencoded (`[{address, storage_keys}]`) formats, normalizing snake_case `storage_keys` to camelCase `storageKeys`
- Updated `createTransactionByType()` — changed hardcoded `accessList: []` to `accessList: fields.accessList ?? []` for types 1, 2, and 4
- Updated `createTransactionFromContractResult()` — now calls `formatAccessList(cr.access_list)`

### `src/relay/lib/types/mirrorNode.ts`
- Widened `access_list` type from `string` to `string | Array<{ address: string; storage_keys: string[] }> | null`

### `tests/relay/lib/factories/transactionFactory.spec.ts`
- Added 10 new unit tests for `formatAccessList`
- Updated 2 existing tests to reflect new behavior

## Backward Compatibility

| MirrorNode `access_list` value | Relay `accessList` result | Status |
|-------------------------------|--------------------------|--------|
| `"0x"` (current) | `[]` | ✅ No change |
| `null` | `[]` | ✅ No change |
| `[]` | `[]` | ✅ No change |
| `[{address, storage_keys}]` (future) | `[{address, storageKeys}]` | ✅ New |

## Related Issues

- Related to hiero-ledger/hiero-mirror-node#13343

## Tests

- [x] 36/36 factory tests passing
- [x] 10 new unit tests for `formatAccessList`
- [x] No regressions in existing tests
Signed-off-by: Manmath Mohanty <manmath.2006n@gmail.com>

issues.title: Bug: Workflow fails with YAML parsing error when checking for `skill: beginner` label

labels.name: skill: beginner
labels.description: Achievable by a fairly new comer that has already completed a couple of good first issues

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The `.github/workflows/request-triage-review.yml` workflow fails immediately with an `Invalid workflow file` error on line 17 when attempting to add the `skill: beginner` label to the `if` condition.

The current line looks like this:
`if: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Good First Issue') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'beginner') }}`

If we try to add `contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skill: beginner')` inside the `${{ }}` wrapper, GitHub Actions throws a YAML syntax error because the colon `:` inside `'skill: beginner'` causes the YAML parser to interpret the string as a key-value pair map rather than a valid expression.

**Proposed Solution**
GitHub Actions evaluates `if` conditions as expressions by default, so we can safely remove the `${{ }}` syntax. This solves the YAML parsing error and allows us to check for labels with colons in them.

Change line 17 to:
`if: contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Good First Issue') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skill: beginner') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'beginner')`

issues.title: Add theme toggle (light/dark mode switch) to the  landing page
issues.body: Feature Request: Add Light/Dark Mode Toggle 🌞 / 🌙 Problem: The DrawDB-io landing page currently supports only one theme (Light ). While it looks great, some users may find it less comfortable or harder to read depending on their lighting conditions. Proposed Solution: Add a simple theme toggle (🌞 / 🌙) in the top-right corner to switch between light and dark modes. Keep dark mode as the default. Save the user’s choice in localStorage and optionally follow system preferences. Slightly refine UI colors for a clean, polished look. Why It Matters: This small update improves accessibility, comfort, and overall user experience — giving visitors control over how they view the site. Note: I’ve already worked on this feature and can open a PR once approved — please assign it to me! 🙌
